page: Microsoft Azure L8s_v4 is a storage-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Lsv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and is equipped with a 260 MB L3 cache. Memory capacity is 64.0 GB, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes 1920 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core capabilities, with the Geekbench single-core score reaching 2168.0 and single-threaded gzip compression performing at 31.37 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25 percent of tested servers. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 7995.24 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8333.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 621.16 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 65.68 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. For LLM workloads, the server demonstrates strong text generation speeds on small models at 270.03 tokens/sec but exhibits weak performance on medium-sized models, dropping to 8.36 tokens/sec for text generation.

Given its high local storage density of 1920 GB alongside 64.0 GB of RAM, the L8s_v4 offers a balanced resource profile for storage-intensive and database workloads. The inclusion of advanced CPU instruction sets, such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions, enhances its qualitative cost efficiency for vector and matrix operations without requiring dedicated GPU resources. This server is suited for relational databases, NoSQL databases, caching layers like Redis, and software compilation tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure L8s_v4 is an x86_64 storage-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 1920 GB of bundled local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance supports Premium Storage and advanced CPU instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks reveal strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2168.0 and single-threaded gzip compression of 31.37 GB/sec. Multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier, with Redis SET operations reaching 11.48 million operations per second. Lacking a GPU, the instance is less suited for large-scale LLM generation but represents a highly efficient option for database operations, caching, and local storage-heavy workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L8s_v4 is an 8-vCPU storage-optimized server with an Intel Xeon 8573C CPU, 64 GB RAM, and 1920 GB local storage, designed for high-throughput database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L8s_v4 features 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1920 GB local storage, delivering strong single-core performance for database workloads.
tagline: An 8-vCPU storage-optimized instance featuring 1920 GB of local storage and high-frequency Intel Xeon processing for database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
64.0 GB of system memory paired with 1920 GB of bundled local storage
Strong single-core performance with a Geekbench score of 2168.0
Equipped with Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector math
Optimized for database operations, Redis caching, and storage-heavy applications
